title: Happy Number
description:
A **happy number** is defined by this process: replace the number with the sum
of the squares of its digits, and repeat until the number equals `1` (happy) or
it loops endlessly in a cycle that never reaches `1`.

Given `n`, return `true` if it is a happy number, and `false` otherwise.

solutions.summary: Track seen values; if one repeats before reaching 1, it loops forever.

steps.title: Loop until 1 or a repeat
steps.explanation: Remember every number we have seen; stop when we hit `1` or revisit a value.

steps.title: Replace n with its digit-square sum
steps.explanation: Peel off each digit, square it, and accumulate the total as the next `n`.

code.python:
def is_happy(n):
    seen = set()
    while n != 1 and n not in seen:
        seen.add(n)
        total = 0
        while n > 0:
            d = n % 10
            total += d * d
            n //= 10
        n = total
    return n == 1
